applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile(mode='default', dynamic=False)
applied_optimizations.description: Applied torch.compile with mode='default' and dynamic=False for ROCm stability. Provides 1.42x speedup through graph optimization, operator fusion, and inductor optimizations. Triton convolution kernels fail during autotuning but model falls back to native convolutions gracefully.

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile(mode='default') with dynamic=True
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton compilation fails on ROCm with 'ttg.async_copy_global_to_local' legalization error
attempted_but_failed.error: failed to legalize operation 'ttg.async_copy_global_to_local' that was explicitly marked illegal in triton_convolution2d kernel

notes: Stage 1 complete: Baseline 2.57ms, Current best 1.82ms (1.42x speedup, SCORE: 64.2)
torch.compile with dynamic=False is the working configuration for ROCm
Triton convolution autotuning fails on ROCm gfx950 with async copy errors
Profiling identifies convolutions (44.9%), ReLU (18.7%), and pooling (10.0%) as optimization targets
Model: GoogleNet InceptionV1 with 11 Inception modules, 570 conv calls total
Next stages should explore: manual Triton kernels for elementwise fusion, Inception module restructuring
